task_completion.description: Open the symptom tracking data in a spreadsheet application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) is open, displaying health data with columns such as Date, Time, Severity, Symptoms_Text, and Possible_Trigger.

task_completion.description: Add a helper column for the day of the week.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column in the spreadsheet (e.g., titled 'Day of Week' or similar) containing days (e.g., Monday, Tuesday) or numeric day representations corresponding to the Date column.

task_completion.description: Add a helper column for time intervals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column (e.g., titled 'Time Interval', 'Time of Day') containing categorized time periods (e.g., Morning, Afternoon, Evening) based on the Time column.

task_completion.description: Add a helper column for weekend classification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column (e.g., titled 'Weekend', 'Is Weekend') containing indicators (e.g., True/False, Yes/No, Weekend/Weekday) classifying whether the date falls on a weekend.

A section in the spreadsheet showing calculated statistics (e.g., average severity, total episode counts). The formula bar should ideally show a formula (e.g., =AVERAGE(), =COUNT(), =COUNTIF()) when a summary cell is selected.
The spreadsheet application's title bar shows 'symptom_analysis.xlsx', or a 'Save As' dialog/file manager confirms the file is saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory with the .xlsx extension.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI spreadsheet application to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a spreadsheet GUI (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) being used, rather than terminal commands, Python scripts, or text editors.

integrity.description: The agent used formulas for calculations rather than manually typing the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar in the spreadsheet shows actual functions (e.g., =IF(), =AVERAGE(), =WEEKDAY()) rather than hardcoded text or numbers for the helper columns and statistics.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file properly through the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is saved via the 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog in the spreadsheet application, ensuring it is a genuine .xlsx file and not just a renamed text/CSV file in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 13 synthetic symptom log entries spanning 28 days. Original fields include Date, Time, Severity (1-10, with 2-3 intentional blanks), Symptoms_Text, and Possible_Trigger. The agent must add three specific helper columns (day of week, time intervals, weekend classification) and calculate summary statistics (which may include episode counts, severity averages, or date spans) using spreadsheet formulas.
